findings.message: 404 warning log does not explicitly include a correlation_id. If ObservabilityMiddleware does not bind a request-scoped correlation_id into structlog contextvars before route dispatch, this warning event will be untrackable across service boundaries and the log→trace link is broken for the not-found path.
findings.suggestion: Confirm ObservabilityMiddleware calls structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars(correlation_id=...) on every request before yielding to the route. If not, pass it explicitly: _log.warning('archive_item_not_found', item_id=item_id, correlation_id=request.headers.get('x-request-id', '')).

findings.message: session.commit() failure path is unobserved. If the commit raises (e.g., IntegrityError, stale connection), the exception propagates with no structured log capturing item_id or mutation intent. The registered exception handler will log a generic 500 with no route-level context, making the incident untriageable without a full trace.
findings.suggestion: Add _log.debug('archive_item_committing', item_id=item_id) immediately before session.commit(), or wrap the commit in a try/except that logs item_id and re-raises, so every exit path from the mutation is observable.

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined for the new archive write endpoint. The fleet-wide request_latency_p99_ms and error_rate_pct in slo.py will absorb archive requests, but POST /items/{item_id}/archive includes a session.commit() and is structurally higher-latency than GET /items. A latency regression on this endpoint can silently inflate the fleet p99 without triggering a targeted alert.
findings.suggestion: Add an endpoint-scoped SLO entry (e.g., key='archive_latency_p99_ms') to slo.py default_slos() and wire a corresponding PromQL expression in provisioning.py, or document explicitly why the shared fleet threshold is an acceptable ceiling for commit-path latency.

findings.message: item_id is not set as an application-level span attribute on the auto-instrumented trace. FastAPIInstrumentor records http.route and http.target (which includes the raw URL), but there is no app.item_id attribute, so filtering traces for a specific item in Tempo/Jaeger requires full-text URL matching rather than a typed attribute lookup. Log↔trace correlation by item is impossible without it.
findings.suggestion: Add opentelemetry.trace.get_current_span().set_attribute('app.item_id', item_id) at the top of archive_item to enrich the auto-created span with a queryable application attribute.

findings.message: Re-archiving an already-archived item silently produces '[archived] [archived] item_name' with no warning log and no 4xx response. This non-idempotent mutation is invisible in both logs and metrics, making it impossible to detect or alert on repeated archive operations causing data drift.
findings.suggestion: Guard with if item.name.startswith('[archived]'): emit _log.warning('archive_item_already_archived', item_id=item_id) and raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail='Item already archived'), or make the operation explicitly idempotent and document that contract.
